answer:
In the Diwan-E-Ghalib, Diwan-E-Zauq, and Bang-E-Dara collections, *ishq* (عشق) is depicted not as mere romantic affection but as an all-consuming passion—often a tormenting "sickness" (*maraz-e-ishq*, *dard-e-ishq*) blending earthly desire with spiritual ecstasy, obsession, and self-annihilation. It defies cure, rationality, or superficial display, demanding total surrender, whether to a beloved or the Divine.[^1][^2]

### Ghalib (Diwan-E-Ghalib)
Ghalib equates *ishq* with *vehshat* (wildness/madness), a relentless force that builds the beloved's fame through the lover's suffering, yet persists despite rejection or torment. It is a profound constancy (*istiqamat*) that heaven cannot fully extinguish, akin to a fatal illness even the Messiah (*Isa*) cannot heal.[^3][^4][^5]

> If not your love, this wild obsession—so be it;  
> My obsession only builds your fame—then so be it.[^6]

### Zauq (Diwan-E-Zauq)
For Zauq, *ishq* is life's central "game" (*ishq-baazi*), a maddening affliction (*maraz*) that erases all other memories, leaving the lover enthralled and oblivious to the world, God, or self. It thrives on cruelty and forgetfulness, reducing existence to the beloved's thrall.[^7][^8]

> When love's a sickness taking hold, what can the mind enthrall?  
> No thought of cure remains, no prayer escapes the fall, and still recall.[^9]

### Iqbal (Bang-E-Dara)
Iqbal elevates *ishq* to a sacred, hidden anguish (*dard-e-ishq*)—a "pearl of purest water" unfit for the unworthy or modern superficiality. It seeks divine union's "final reach" (*inteha*), embracing torment over paradise, as a transformative fire hidden in the heart.[^10][^11]

> O, Anguish of Love! A pearl of purest water, you;  
> Be not revealed to the unworthy view.[^12]

> The final reach of Your Love is what I seek;  
> Behold my simplicity, what I beseech is what I seek.[^13]

While motifs overlap (pain, madness, divinity), Ghalib emphasizes defiant persistence, Zauq the mind-erasing malady, and Iqbal its concealed spiritual pinnacle. The corpus lacks exhaustive philosophical treatises on *ishq*, focusing instead on poetic evocation.

[^1]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [chapter](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/84-ishq-mujh-ko-nahin-vehshat-hi-sahi/translation/).
[^2]: Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq*, [chapter](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/35-maraz-e-ishq-jise-ho-use-kya-yaad-rahe/translation-2.md).
[^3]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [chapter](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/84-ishq-mujh-ko-nahin-vehshat-hi-sahi/translation/).
[^4]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [chapter](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/85-ishq-taasir-se-na-umeed-nahin/translation-2.md).
[^5]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/126-lo-hum-mareez-e-ishq-ke-beemaar-daar-hain/translation-2.md/#p-cdb14e).
[^6]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/84-ishq-mujh-ko-nahin-vehshat-hi-sahi/translation/#p-a4c81f).
[^7]: Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq*, [chapter](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/20-hum-hain-aur-shughl-e-ishq-baazi-hai/translation/).
[^8]: Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq*, [chapter](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/35-maraz-e-ishq-jise-ho-use-kya-yaad-rahe/translation-2.md).
[^9]: Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq, *Diwan-E-Zauq*, [paragraph](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/35-maraz-e-ishq-jise-ho-use-kya-yaad-rahe/translation-2.md/#p-32ad70).
[^10]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/17-dard-e-ishq/translation-2.md).
[^11]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/12-tere-ishq-ki-inteha-chahta-hoon/translation/).
[^12]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/17-dard-e-ishq/translation-2.md/#p-6d19d8).
[^13]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/12-tere-ishq-ki-inteha-chahta-hoon/translation/#p-34168f).
